methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Roboneers is a Growth-stage company in the Defense & Security sector founded by Anton and Oleksiy Skrypnyk. The company has raised $2M+ to date. The Defense & Security sector comprises 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Shield AI, and Flock Safety—though most lack direct stage and valuation alignment with Roboneers.

Given the scarcity of precise comparables at Roboneers' stage and valuation tier within Defense & Security, peer-median analysis was not applicable. Instead, stage-progression methodology was employed as the primary valuation approach. Using the $2M total raised as a proxy for base value and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for Growth-stage advancement, this method implies a valuation of $2.4M.

The triangulated range supports a median valuation of $2.4M, with a low-end floor near $2M and a high-end ceiling around $2.8M. The single-method basis of this range reflects a core limitation: insufficient narrow-peer data within Defense & Security at comparable stages. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and defensibility of technology—factors not captured in raised capital alone. A more robust valuation would benefit from revenue multiples or customer acquisition metrics once available.
